title: podman log needs to fail if the log file does not exists.
body:
Currently if you create a container and then do a podman log on it, it just
hangs.  This change will get the container to exit with an error saying the
log file does not exists.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2677

@edsantiago @mheon @baude @vrothberg @giuseppe PTAL

This only question I have on this, is whether we should set the MustExists flag if we specify --follow?  
Currently if you create a container and never run it, there are no logs.  With current podman logs, it hangs if the file does not exists. With podman logs --follow, we will hang when there is a log, but exits when you execute without the --follow.
@dwalsh PTAL at #2679; @baude has a working fix already; his preserves the existing (and IMHO correct) behavior of exiting zero.
